debug_info.goal:
x y : BitVec (4 + 4)
⊢ x ^^^ y = y ^^^ x

failed_attempts:
apply Eq.symm
    apply Eq.symm
    -- Use the property of XOR being commutative for each bit
    apply BitVec.ext
    intro i
    simp [BitVec.xor_apply, xor_comm]
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]
    <;>
    omega
  exact h

-- Use the symmetry of the XOR operation, which is already a known property in Lean's BitVec library.
    rw [← BitVec.xor_comm]
    <;> simp_all [BitVec.xor_assoc]
  exact h

apply Eq.symm
    apply Eq.symm
    -- Use the fact that two bit vectors are equal if all their corresponding bits are equal
    apply BitVec.ext
    intro i
    -- For each bit position i, the XOR operation is commutative
    simp [BitVec.xor_apply, Bool.xor_comm]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try simp_all) <;>
    (try aesop)
  
  apply h_main

simp [Nat.xor_comm] at * <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try omega) <;>
  (try ring_nf at *) <;>
  (try aesop)

cases y with | mk y =>
  apply Eq.symm
  <;> simp [Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_left_comm, BitVec.xor_def, Nat.xor_comm]
  <;> rfl

have h₁ : x = y := by
      ext i
      <;> simp [BitVec.ext_iff]
      <;> decide
    rw [h₁]
  exact h

mul := (^^^)
  -- Use the symmetry of XOR to prove commutativity
  have h : ∀ (a b : BitVec (4 + 4)), a ^^^ b = b ^^^ a := by
    intro a b
    apply Eq.symm
    <;>
    simp [xor_comm]
  -- Apply the commutativity property to the specific case of x and y
  apply h
